operating_state: Total revenues increased 12% to $90.2 billion driven by Google Search acceleration and Google Cloud growing 28%. Google advertising revenues reached $66.9 billion with YouTube ads expanding from increased direct response and brand advertising, while Google Network declined. Operating cash flows surged to $36.2 billion from $28.8 billion driven by higher cash receipts across advertising, subscriptions, and cloud consumption-based fees.
cost_structure: Cost of revenues increased 8% to $36.4 billion while gross margin improved to 60% as revenue growth outpaced cost increases from content acquisition, traffic acquisition, and depreciation. Capital expenditures surged to $17.2 billion for technical infrastructure supporting AI products and services. Research and development expenses rose while depreciation increased as prior data center investments reached service readiness.
material_events: Entered definitive agreement to acquire Wiz cloud security platform for $32.0 billion in all-cash transaction expected to close in 2026. Board authorized additional $70 billion share repurchase program following $15.3 billion in Q1 repurchases. Quarterly dividend increased 5% to $0.21 per share with $2.5 billion total distribution. EU General Court overturned 2019 decision and annulled €1.5 billion fine though EC has appealed.
macro_exposures: International revenues representing 51% of total faced unfavorable foreign currency impacts with U.S. dollar strengthening reducing reported growth by approximately 2 percentage points. Outstanding European Commission competition fines total approximately €4.1 billion under appeal. OECD minimum global tax rate implementation had no material current period effect.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued technical infrastructure investment increases supporting business growth and long-term AI initiatives with revenue backlog of $92.4 billion indicating 55% recognition over next 24 months. Wiz acquisition upon closing will integrate into Google Cloud segment to enhance cybersecurity offerings. Company maintains $29.5 billion remaining under current authorization plus new $70 billion share repurchase authorization.
uncertainty_profile: Multiple antitrust proceedings remain pending including U.S. District Court remedies hearing scheduled April 2025 following adverse Search ruling with DOJ proposing structural remedies that could materially affect operations. European Commission Digital Markets Act investigations issued preliminary non-compliance findings in March 2025. Outstanding purchase commitments total $62.1 billion primarily for technical infrastructure and content licenses, while long-term tax liabilities of $9.8 billion relate to uncertain tax positions.
